title: Sum of Array Elements
description: Given an integer array `runs`, return the sum of all its elements. Solve it recursively without using any loops.
hints: Base case: empty ya last index reach kiya — return 0.
Recurrence: sum(arr, i) = arr[i] + sum(arr, i+1).
Tu helper function bana sakta hai jo index pass kare.

title: Factorial
description: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return its factorial `n!` (the product of all positive integers up to `n`). Implement the computation recursively. By convention, `0! = 1`.
hints: Base case: n === 0 ya n === 1 → return 1.
Recurrence: factorial(n) = n * factorial(n-1).
Memoization tab useful jab same n bar bar pucha jaaye.

title: Fibonacci Number
description: Given an integer `n`, return the n-th Fibonacci number, where `fib(0) = 0`, `fib(1) = 1`, and `fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)` for `n >= 2`. Define the result recursively; memoize repeated subproblems for efficiency.
hints: Base: n < 2 → return n.
Recurrence: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2).
Plain recursion exponential hai — memo se O(n) hota hai.

title: Print Numbers Forward and Backward
description: Given an integer `n`, return an object `{forward, reverse}` where `forward` is the array `[1, 2, ..., n]` and `reverse` is the array `[n, n-1, ..., 1]`. Build both sequences using recursion only, without any loops.
hints: Forward: print first, then recurse on n+1 — but tu reverse direction se bhi solve kar sakta hai.
Trick: `printForward(n)` = `printForward(n-1)` then push n. Stack unwinding order use kar.
Reverse: push n first, then recurse with n-1.

title: Sum of Digits
description: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return the sum of its decimal digits. Compute the result recursively.
hints: Base: n === 0 → return 0.
Recurrence: sum(n) = (n % 10) + sum(floor(n/10)).
Iterative form bhi simple hai using while loop.

title: Reverse String
description: Given a string `s`, return the string reversed. Solve it using recursion only, without loops or any built-in reverse function.
hints: Base: empty ya single char string return as-is.
Recurrence: reverse(s) = reverse(s.slice(1)) + s[0].
Linear in length — har call ek char strip kare.

title: Pow(x, n)
description: Given a base `x` and an integer exponent `n`, return `x` raised to the power `n` (i.e. `x^n`). Implement fast exponentiation recursively using divide-and-conquer (repeated squaring). When `n` is negative, return `1 / x^|n|`.
hints: Recurrence: agar n even → power(x, n/2)^2. Odd → x * power(x, n-1).
Negative n: convert to 1 / power(x, -n) at top level.

title: Tower of Hanoi
description: You are given `n` disks stacked on rod `A` and must move them all to rod `C`, using rod `B` as auxiliary, subject to the rule that a larger disk may never sit on a smaller one. Given `n`, return an object `{count, moves}`, where `count` is the minimum number of moves required and `moves` is the sequence of moves as strings of the form `"A->C"`. Solve it recursively.
hints: Base: n === 1 → just move from source to dest.
Recurrence: move n-1 from src→aux, move n-th from src→dest, move n-1 from aux→dest.
Total moves count = 2^n - 1.

title: Recursive Palindrome Check
description: Given a string `s`, return `true` if `s` reads the same forwards and backwards, and `false` otherwise. The comparison is case-sensitive. Solve it using pure recursion.
hints: Base: length <= 1 → true.
Recurrence: agar s[0] !== s[n-1] → false; else recurse on s[1..n-2].
Two-pointer recursive form O(n) time and O(n) stack.

title: Decimal to Binary Conversion
description: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return its binary representation as a string with no leading zeros. If `n` is `0`, return `"0"`. Build the string recursively.
hints: Base: n === 0 → return "0" at TOP-LEVEL ONLY; in recursion 0 should return empty.
Recurrence: binary(n) = binary(floor(n/2)) + (n % 2).
Tu wrapper function bana ke top-level pe handle kar le n=0 ka edge.

title: Greatest Common Divisor
description: Given two non-negative integers `a` and `b`, return their greatest common divisor using the Euclidean algorithm, implemented recursively. By convention, `gcd(0, 0) = 0`.
hints: Base: b === 0 → return a.
Recurrence: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a % b).
a < b ka case automatically pehle swap ho jaata hai (a%b == a).

title: String Permutations
description: Given a string `s` consisting of distinct characters (length at most 7), return all of its permutations as an array sorted in lexicographic order. Generate the permutations recursively.
hints: Base: length 1 → return [s].
Recurrence: take s[0], permute s[1..], insert s[0] at every position of each smaller permutation.
Final result ko sort kar do lexicographically.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given an integer `n`, return all combinations of `n` pairs of well-formed (balanced) parentheses, as an array sorted in lexicographic order. Use a recursive function that tracks the number of open and close parentheses still available along with the string built so far.
hints: State: (open_left, close_left, current_string).
Base: open_left === 0 && close_left === 0 → push current_string.
Branches: agar open_left > 0 → add '('. Agar close_left > open_left → add ')'.

title: Unique Paths
description: You start at the top-left cell of an `m x n` grid and want to reach the bottom-right cell, moving only right or down at each step. Return the number of unique paths. Solve it recursively with memoization.
hints: State: (row, col). Base: pohunch gaya at (m-1, n-1) → 1 path.
Recurrence: paths(r, c) = paths(r+1, c) + paths(r, c+1).
Memoize using 2D map ya array taaki same (r,c) bar bar na compute ho.

title: Reverse a Stack Using Recursion
description: Given a stack represented as an array `stack` (where the last element is the top), return the stack reversed so that the original bottom element becomes the new top. You must use recursion only, with the call stack as your sole auxiliary memory — no extra array, list, or second stack is allowed.
hints: Two recursive helpers: reverse(stack) aur insertAtBottom(stack, x).
reverse: pop top, recursively reverse rest, fir insertAtBottom me use kar.
insertAtBottom: agar empty → push x. Else pop, recurse, fir wapas push.
